message:
fcp-store: content-id verifier closes attacker-chosen object_id injection

## Why

Bead flywheel_connectors-4g0qr: `StoredObject::object_id` is NORMATIVELY
defined as the keyed-BLAKE3 hash
`ObjectId::new(canonical_bytes(header, body), zone_id, schema, ObjectIdKey)`.
Neither the runtime API (`MemoryObjectStore::put`) nor the durable
replay path (`DurableObjectStore::open` → `load_durable_object_state`
→ `from_snapshot` / `apply_loaded_mutation` Put branch) verified that
invariant — they trusted whatever `object_id` the caller, the WAL, or
the snapshot claimed.

Concrete attack: a process with write access to `objects.wal.jsonl`
(compromised host, restored-from-tampered-backup, malicious
snapshot import) appends `Put(StoredObject { object_id: H, header: H',
body: B' })` where `(H', B')` are attacker-chosen bytes under a claimed
legit id `H`. The WAL envelope checksum covers only the outer
`(version, seq, op)` tuple, so the on-disk integrity check accepts
the forged record. On restart the replay loop `insert_loaded`s the
forged bytes and subsequent `get(H)` calls return the attacker's
`(H', B')`.

The storage layer cannot verify the keyed hash by itself because the
zone-specific `ObjectIdKey` lives in `ZoneKeyMaterial`, outside the
store. So the defense is optional — callers that hold zone-key
material install a verifier at the store boundary.

## What

New `crates/fcp-store/src/object_id_verifier.rs` module:

  - `pub trait ObjectIdVerifier: Send + Sync + Debug` with a single
    `verify(&StoredObject) -> Result<(), ObjectStoreError>` method
    that MUST fail closed on unknown zones.
  - `pub struct KeyedObjectIdVerifier { keys: HashMap<ZoneId, ObjectIdKey> }`
    — concrete impl that recomputes `StoredObject::derive_id(header,
    body, key)` and compares to the claimed id.

Two new `ObjectStoreError` variants carry the diagnostics:

  - `ContentIdMismatch { claimed, computed }` — detection path.
  - `VerifierKeyMissing { zone }` — fail-closed on unknown zones.

Wire-up at every trust boundary:

  - `MemoryObjectStore`: `.with_verifier(Arc<dyn ObjectIdVerifier>)`
    builder; `put()` calls `verifier.verify(&object)` after
    `validate_structure` but before the lock.
  - `DurableObjectStore::open_with_verifier(config, verifier)`:
    threads an `Option<Arc<dyn ObjectIdVerifier>>` through
    `load_durable_object_state` → `from_snapshot` (every snapshot
    entry) and the WAL replay loop (every `ObjectWalOp::Put`
    record). `record_mutation` checks the live runtime path.
  - `open` remains as a convenience that passes `None` — existing
    single-node / no-key callers unchanged.

Ordering: the verifier runs BEFORE `validate_mutation`'s
`contains_key` duplicate check on both the runtime and the replay
paths. A forged record that reuses a legit id must surface as the
more specific `ContentIdMismatch`, not as `AlreadyExists` (which
would mask the real defect).

## Tests

Six new tests — four unit tests for the verifier primitive and two
integration tests at the store boundaries:

  - `object_id_verifier::tests`:
    - `accepts_genuine_object_with_correct_content_id`
    - `rejects_forged_object_id_even_with_correct_header_and_body`
    - `rejects_tampered_body_with_preserved_id`
    - `unknown_zone_fails_closed_with_verifier_key_missing`
  - `object_store::tests::put_with_verifier_rejects_forged_object_id`
    — verifier installed on `MemoryObjectStore`, `put` of a forged id
    surfaces `ContentIdMismatch`, `get(forged_id)` returns
    `NotFound`, `storage_used` stays 0.
  - `durable::tests::wal_replay_with_verifier_rejects_forged_object_id`
    — writes a legit WAL record, appends a forged envelope (with
    correct outer checksum) that reuses the legit id but carries a
    different body, reopens with the verifier, asserts
    `ContentIdMismatch` surfaces (NOT `AlreadyExists`, which would
    indicate the verifier was bypassed by the dup check).

## Verified

  CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-p2-r1 cargo test -p fcp-store
  → 795 + 88 + 14 = 897 pass, 0 fail
  → six new tests all green

Downstream `fcp-mesh` + `fcp-conformance` still build cleanly. No
existing caller sees behaviour changes (verifier is opt-in via
`with_verifier` / `open_with_verifier`).

Closes flywheel_connectors-4g0qr.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
